qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF via dataset import data URI allows fetching arbitrary internal URLs
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable component and root cause: this finding is dataset import URL validation leading to urlopen SSRF, not PostgreSQL SQLLab read-only DML validation bypass.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SQL Lab results endpoint missing ownership verification allows IDOR access to other users query results
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same broad SQLLab area but different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding is a missing results ownership check, not a PostgreSQL read-only query/DML validation bypass.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described root cause: an authenticated SQLLab user bypassing read-only verification for PostgreSQL connections with specially crafted DML SQL. The qualifying findings cover dataset import SSRF and SQLLab results IDOR instead.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-mwf2-qr4v-94h2 describes improper input validation in Apache Superset PostgreSQL connections that lets authenticated SQLLab users bypass read-only verification for specially crafted DML statements.
ssrf_via_dataset_import targets POST /api/v1/dataset/import/ and superset/commands/dataset/importers/v1/utils.py URL fetching, which is unrelated to SQLLab PostgreSQL read-only enforcement.

sqllab_results_idor targets GET /api/v1/sqllab/results/ and SqlExecutionResultsCommand missing ownership verification, which is unrelated to read-only DML detection or PostgreSQL connection validation.
